name: Tarte Au Citron
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll out half the dough 1/8-inch thick on a floured surface.
2. Press it firmly into an 8-inch tart pan with a removable fluted rim.
3. Trim off excess dough, leaving a 1/2-inch overhang.
4. Fold the overhang back inside the rim and press it to the side of the shell.
5. Push the dough up 1/4-inch above the edge of the pan. Crimp the edge decoratively with a fork.
6. Roll out remaining dough and make another tart shell in the same manner.
7. Chill the shells for 60 minutes.
8. Line the shells with foil, fill the foil with rice and bake in the lower third of a preheated 400°F oven for 8 minutes.
9. Remove the rice and foil, and bake for 8-10 minutes more or until they are pale golden. Remove shells from the pans, transfer them to a rack and let them cool.
1. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar until the mixture is thick and pale and forms a ribbon.
2. Beat in zest and lemon juice.
3. Transfer the mixture to a heavy saucepan. Cook it, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon over medium-low heat, until it registers 140°F on a candy thermometer.
4. Continue to cook at a stable 140°F for 3 minutes longer. Do not let it boil.
5. Transfer the mixture to a bowl and let it cool, covered with a buttered round of wax paper, until it is lukewarm.
1. In a bowl, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until they hold soft peaks.
2. Add the remaining 1/4 cup sugar, one tablespoon at a time, and beat the meringue until it just holds stiff peaks.
3. Stir a quarter of the meringue into the lemon mixture and fold in the remaining meringue gently but thoroughly.
1. Transfer tart shells to a baking sheet and fill with the lemon mixture, mounding slightly.
2. Bake tarts in the middle of a preheated 400°F oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the tops are golden and puffed.
3. Transfer tarts to a rack and let them cool.
